item.title: Rickenbacker 4003S Bass 2026 - JetGlo (Black) — $1,949
item.description:
$1,949 · Brand New · Andy Babiuk's Fab Gear

ON SALE FOR A LIMITED TIME - July 1st - 30th

NEW – This traditional Rickenbacker bass style with dot inlays and no binding was prized by Paul McCartney and Chris Squire when they got their first 4001S models in 1964. Players cherish its traditional look and rounded edges that many claim make it more comfortable than the 4003.

Of course, the famous solid bass tones, ringing sustain and treble punch Rickenbacker is known for make it a dream. It’s no wonder they’ve stood the test of time, and been a favorite of players across a wide range of genres for decades. An official Rickenbacker Deluxe Hardshell Case is included.

This design featuring dot inlays and no binding was once exclusive to the British distributor Rose Morris in the sixties and made this model stand out from the standard 4003. Iconic curves of maple form the body, with a traditional Rosewood fingerboard adorning the neck. The 4003S now features many modern improvements players have been asking for: a thinner, early 70’s style neck profile with an improved bi-flex trussrod system, and a fully adjustable tailpiece, allowing for greater control of string height and increased sustain.

Other improvements include the new V.2 bass bridge pickup, delivering the same iconic Rickenbacker bass tone we all love, now with a newly designed pickup cover enabling you to effortlessly access the strings from any position on the body. This bass comes with its official Rickenbacker Deluxe Hardshell Case.

Note: Photos of stock instrument, Contact us for photos of exact serial number you'll receive.

IN STOCK AND READY TO SHIP!

Includes free shipping and free expert guitar setup!


SPECS

Neck

  • No. Frets: 20
  • Scale Length: 33.25” (84.5 cm)
  • Neck Width at Nut: 1.6875” (42.9 mm)
  • Neck Width at 12th Fret: 2.125” (54.0 mm)
  • Crown Radius: 10” (25.4 cm)
  • Truss Rod: Single Bi-flex
  • Truss Rod Cover: White Modern Style Rickenbacker Nameplate
  • Neck Type: Through body
  • Fret Marker Style: Dot Pearloid
  • Side Dots: Black
  • Fingerboard Wood: Rosewood
  • Neck Wood: Maple with Walnut Stripe
  • Neck Binding: None


Body

  • Body Type: Solid
  • Body Wood: Maple with Walnut Stripe
  • Body Style: Cresting wave
  • Binding: None

Hardware

  • Tailpiece/Bridge: RIC V.2 Bridge Tailpiece
  • Machine Heads: Schaller Deluxe

Electronics

  • No. of Pickups: 2
  • Type of Pickups: Single Coil
  • Active/Passive Pickups: Passive
  • Neck Pickup: Rickenbacker Hi-Gain Bass Pickup
  • Bridge Pickup: Rickenbacker V.2. Hi-Gain Bass Bridge Pickup
  • Output Type: Mono
  • Pickup Toggle Switch: 3-Way
  • Push Pull Switch: Vintage Tone Selector Circuit


Overall Dimensions

  • Weight: 9.0 lbs. (4.1 kg)
  • Overall Length: 44” (113.8 cm )
  • Overall Width: 13 1/2” (34.3 cm)
  • Overall Depth: 1 1/4” (31.8 mm)


Misc.

  • Case Type: Deluxe Rickenbacker Hardshell Case
  • Also Included: Polishing cloth, Warranty Information, Collectible Rickenbacker sticker, Owners Manual.

item.title: FENDER American Vintage II 1966 Jazz Bass, Rosewood Fingerboard, Lake Placid Blue — $2,723.57
item.description:
$2,723.57 · Brand New · Diplomate Music Center

The Fender® American Vintage II series presents a remarkably accurate take on the revolutionary designs that altered the course of musical history. Built with period-accurate bodies, necks and hardware, premium finishes and meticulously voiced, year-specific pickups, each instrument captures the essence of authentic Fender craftsmanship and tone.

When Don Randall at Fender Sales requested a more sophisticated, high-end bass model, the now iconic Jazz Bass® was the result. The neck was narrower at the nut, providing a faster and more effortless feel and the sleek new body style mirrored the offset contoured style of the Jazzmaster®. By 1966, evolution of the instrument reached new heights with striking new features.

The American Vintage II 1966 Jazz Bass features a comfortable "U" shape hard rock maple neck, and it plays like a dream with a 7.25" radius round-lam rosewood fingerboard and vintage tall frets. Characteristic of production in 1966, this J-Bass® is offered with white neck binding, pearl dots, two volume controls with master tone, matching painted headstock and 4-ply tortoiseshell pickguard. The 4-saddle bridge with threaded saddles, vintage-style "lollipop" tuners and Pure Vintage '66 Jazz Bass pickups deliver classic thump and style.

The instruments in the American Vintage II series are direct descendants of the original Fenders: designed for players with a fine appreciation for vintage Fender tone and feel and built with unmatched quality, down to the last screw. These are Fender electrics in their purest form: Fender American Vintage II, the stuff of legends.

Features
Alder Body
Gloss Nitrocellulose Lacquer Finish
Pure Vintage '66 Single-Coil Jazz Bass® Pickups
"U"-Shaped Neck Profile
Bound Round-Lam Rosewood Fretboard with Paint Matched Headstock
Pure Vintage 'Lollipop' Tuners

item.title: Alembic Elan 1990 - White — $3,500
item.description:
$3,500 · Very Good · Jay Jay's Occasional Bass Listings

I’ve got the BBNE2 bug bad and that’s why I’m selling


1990 Elan in all white with gold hardware/brass hardware Serial 90H-5873
Inclined to believe this is an all maple build with ebony board and pearloid inlays, brass side dots
Neck through.
About 9.8lbs 
PJ set up with Vol, Blend, Filter sweep and Q switch. 
All hard ware and Truss rods work and adjust (yes it has two truss rods, not as scary to work with as you might think) 
Semi fresh set of Dr sunbeams on. 
Aftermarket Hiscox case 

The simple one knob filter and q switch is surprisingly versatile and makes this an Alembic you could actually easily gig. 

*disclaimer I am not sure if this is the original paint job. It’s pretty good, I brought it in to Chuck Levins in Wheaton and long time salesman Paul Schein swore he remembered ordering and selling this bass for Chuck Levins back when they were Alembic Dealers, but guitar department manager Carlos didn’t think it was an original paint job. I suppose sending Alembic an email to see if they have it in the books as an all white build is the easiest way to clear this up. I’ve just been busy this week and haven’t gotten around to it. Looks great though, so I don’t care either way.

item.title: Squier Classic Vibe 60's Jazz Bass 4-String Bass Guitar Burgundy Mist (549) — $399.95
item.description:
$399.95 · Mint · Morrell Music Co.

This 2026 Squier Classic Vibe 60's Jazz Bass is used with "used" stamped on the back of the headstock. It is in mint condition and shows no dings, dents, scratches, or any obvious playwear. This bass has been professionally set up and plays great! The neck is straight, the action is low and comfortable, and the bass chords clearly and easily all the way up the neck. Awesome deal on a great playing and sounding Squier Classic Vibe 60's Jazz Bass!



A tribute to the decade of its birth, the Classic Vibe ‘60s Jazz Bass® combines the luxurious playability that made it famous with the versatility and massive tone of its dual Fender-Designed alnico single-coil pickups. Player-friendly features include a slim, comfortable “C”-shaped neck profile with an easy-playing 9.5”-radius fingerboard and narrow-tall frets, as well as a vintage-style bridge with threaded saddles. This throwback Squier model also features 1960s-inspired headstock markings, rich-looking nickel-plated hardware and a slick vintage-tint gloss neck finish for an old-school vibe.

FEATURES:

Solid Poplar Jazz Bass Body
Fender-Designed Alnico Single Coil Pickup Set
Dual Volume Controls/ Master Tone Control
4-Saddle Vintage-Style Bridge w/ Threaded Steel Saddles
Vintage-Style Tuning Machines
Nickel Hardware
4-Ply Tortoise Shell Pickguard
Maple Neck
Indian Laurel Fretboard
C Shape Neck Profile
20 Narrow Tall Frets
Bone Nut
1.5" Nut Width
9.5" Fretboard Radius
34" Scale Length
White Pearloid Dot Inlays
Tinted Gloss Urethane Neck Finish
Gloss Burgundy Mist Finish

item.title: Fender Standard Precision Bass (2025) 4-String Bass Guitar Gloss Black Finish — $499.95
item.description:
$499.95 · Mint · Morrell Music Co.

This 2025 Fender Standard Precision Bass is used with "used" stamped on the back of the headstock. It is in mint condition and shows no dings, dents, scratches, or any obvious playwear. This bass has been professionally set up and plays great! The neck is straight, the action is low and comfortable, and the frets are in excellent condition. Awesome deal on a fantastic playing and sounding Fender Standard Precision Bass in a gorgeous gloss Black Finish!


THE STANDARD IS SET


Originally released by Fender® in 1951, the Precision Bass® was a groundbreaking invention that allowed the bassists of the day to enjoy the fretted precision and amplified volume that their upright basses lacked, quickly making it the go-to foundational instrument chosen for countless landmark recordings.

Built to accompany players on their formative musical adventures, the Fender Standard Precision Bass delivers the approachable playability and inspiring tone that made Fender an icon of rock & roll. Powered by a Fender Standard pickup for versatile iconic tone and an inviting “Modern C”-shaped neck for superior playing comfort, this P Bass® truly sets the standard for the Fender experience.


FEATURES:

Solid Poplar Precision Bass Body
Gloss Gloss Black Finish
Maple Bolt-On Neck

Modern "C" Neck Profile
34" Scale Length
Indian Laurel Fretboard

Micarta Nut

White Side Dot Markers
Dual Action Truss Rod
Fender Standard Split Coil Precision Bass Pickup
Master Volume/ Master Tone Controls
Fender Standard Top-Load Bridge w/ Steel Barrel Saddles
Chrome Hardware

Squier Sonic Bronco Bass

_x000d_

The Squier Sonic Bronco Bass is ready to launch any musical adventure into warp speed offering iconic Fender style and inspiring tone for players at any stage. This Bronco Bass sports a slim and inviting C shaped neck profile with a narrow 1.5 inch nut width and a thin lightweight body for optimal playing comfort while a Squier single coil pickup delivers punchy bass tone. Further details of this model include a short 30 inch scale length 4 saddle hardtail bridge for precise intonation adjustment sealed gear tuning machines for smooth accurate tuning and durable chrome plated hardware that is sure to catch the spotlight.

item.title: Gretsch 7629 Committee Bass SuperTron Pickup — $1,499.99
item.description:
$1,499.99 · Very Good · Tower Guitar & Bass Shop

EVERYTHING MUST GO! I am retiring from music and all of my instruments are being sold (except for a cheap nylon string to satisfy the urge should it arise). This is everything that I own, please feel free to ask any questions and I will do my best to answer them.


This listing is for a Gretsch Committee Bass and, as you can see by the pictures, it is in good shape. This is all original, with the original case. These kind of have a Thunderbird mixed with a P Bass kind of sound and this one plays great.

The Gretsch Committee Bass (Model 7629) is a USA-made 4-string bass manufactured from 1977 to 1980. Known for its unconventional design, it features a solid walnut symmetrical double-cutaway body, a 34" scale, and a neck-through-body laminated maple/walnut neck. It is equipped with a single SuperTron pickup, controlled by a single volume and tone knob.

Further details of this model include a short 30" scale length, 4-saddle hardtail bridge for precise intonation adjustment, sealed-gear tuning machines for smooth, accurate tuning and durable chrome-plated hardware that is sure to catch the spotlight.

  • Squier single-coil pickup
  • 4-saddle hardtail bridge
  • Sealed-gear tuning machines

item.description:

Vintage Guitars LVJ74 Pro Guitars UNIQUE JAZZ BASS Fretless 2026 Sunburst Gaucher/Zurdo Exclusive Al’s Music Factory

Vintage VJ74 ProShop Unique Left Hand Fretless Bass ~ Nitro Cellulose Sunburst

LVJ74PC0001 Features

Eastern poplar body for improved resonance, tone and ‘correct weight’ comfort, enhanced with correctly radiused contoured body.

Fretless.

Classic Vintage headstock design for balance and visual appeal.

item.title: Fender Precision Bass — $700
item.description:
$700 · Very Good · John H's Gear Outlet

Purchased from The Guitar Center in Chicago in 1994. Fender started making the Standard Precison Bass in Mexico in 1991. Rumor has it that in the begining parts were sent in from the US such as bodies and necks. I used every Sunday in the church chior. Retired this bass to recording studio work 10 years ago. Recently strung with high end Thomas-Infeld nickel flatwound strings, 0.43 to .100. These strings are made in Vienna, Austria. They are super quiet and easy to play. This bass has not been frequently played over all throughout the years. One worn spot on the top side where you typically rest your right arm. Bass wieghts 8.4 lbs with strap. Serial number begins with M (mexico), N (the 1900's) and 4 (the year: 1994). Unfortunately the Fender Serial number lookup is not totally accurate tha this bass did not show up in a search.

item.title: Dingwall John Taylor JT3 5-String Seafoam Green — $2,245
item.description:
$2,245 · Brand New · Ish Guitars

Dingwall John Taylor JT3 5-String Seafoam Green

Weight: 8.9 lbs 

DESCRIPTION

The John Taylor Signature model follows the formula of the limited edition Rio Dream Bass, barring a few design tweaks and the Metallic Black, Primrose and Seafoam Green finishes, as well as a new 5-string variant.

On top of stunning looks and flawless construction, each bass offers wonderful playability and tones that display a rare clarity and refinement. “John’s bass playing with Duran Duran really imprinted on me how a bass should fit into a band mix,” says Sheldon Dingwall. “His basslines have always had a special combination of energy and elegance.”

Like the Rio Dream Bass, the JT Signature has been configured to Taylor’s own personal specifications. “It all started when I was in Toronto about five years ago,” says Taylor. “A friend showed me a Dingwall bass on his phone. I loved how it looked and immediately said to my tech, ‘You’ve got to reach out to these guys!’”

As well as the ‘Rio Eye’ inlaid at the 12th fret, other specs include a lightweight Nyatoh body, a bolt-on Maple neck and a Pau Ferro multi-scale fingerboard. The headstock sports a “Rio-inspired” graphic which complements the body stripes, designed by longtime Duran Duran collaborator, Patty Palazzo. The customary Dingwall fanned-fret system facilitates the most appropriate scale length for each individual string. It’s a concept that’s applied across the entire Dingwall range.

“This arrangement varies the strings’ scale length, meaning that the low B string benefits from maximum tension and feels great to play, with none of the dull-sounding flopping around that you get from a regular-scale 5-string.” Bass Player Magazine

SPECS

Weight: 8.9 lbs

Case: Dingwall Padded Gigbag

Year: 2026

Scale Length: 5-String: 37” - 34”

Body: Nyatoh

Neck: 5-Piece Maple, bolt-on.

Fingerboard: Pau Ferro, 24 ‘banjo-sized’ frets, 9.45-inch radius

Pickups: Dingwall FD3n Pickups, Neodymium Magnets

Radius: 9.45”  (240mm)

Frets: Novax Fanned-fret System, 24 “banjo-sized” frets, 0.040” tall x 0.080” wide

Preamp: Rupert Neve Designs 2-Band EQ - Bass: +/- 12db / 20Hz, Treble: +/- 12db / 4.4KHz

Controls: Master volume, 4-Position Quad-Tone pickup selector, 2-Band Active EQ, Active/Passive Toggle Switch

Bridge: Dingwall ‘Minimalist’ one-piece bridge

String Spacing: 18mm (5-string)

Tuners: Lightweight, open-gear

Nut: Graphtech Black Tusq

SETUP & INSPECTED BY OUR TECH TEAM

All of our guitars come with a FREE professional setup to a comfortable action as specified by that instrument’s manufacturer. Our setups always include inspection of (and necessary adjustment to): action, intonation, tremolo angle, truss rod, frets, nut, bridge, and more. We can accommodate special setup requests - additional charges and/or shipping delays of 1-2 business days could occur.

INDIVIDUALLY PHOTOGRAPHED

After 15 years of innovation in our photography process, Ish Guitars has developed a way to get you the most accurate true-to-life color of every instrument we sell. We never embellish, oversaturate, or misrepresent our images, so that your buying experience can be as much like visiting our showroom as possible. Keep in mind that various computer screens can show colors a bit differently, so the color on your screen may not match the real thing perfectly no matter how hard we try. Also, unless otherwise noted, we individually list and photo every piece of gear that comes through our doors.

HEAR A LIVE DEMO

Experience our innovative Video Appointment service! Connect with our Gear Experts to hear this instrument played live over Zoom and receive one-on-one guidance. Contact us to schedule your appointment!

item.title: Yamaha TRBX305CAR 5-String Bass Guitar Gloss Candy Apple Red Finish — $399.95
item.description:
$399.95 · Mint · Morrell Music Co.

This 2025 Yamaha TRBX305CAR 5-String Bass Guitar has been a demo in our retail store. It is in mint condition and shows no dings, dents, scratches, or any obvious playwear. This bass has been professionally set up and plays great! The neck is straight, the action is low and comfortable, and the frets are in excellent condition. Awesome deal on a great playing and sounding Yamaha TRBX305CAR!

Pickups
The TRBX300's YGD-designed M3 pickups feature oversized polepieces and ceramic magnets for a clear, powerful tone with incredible definition and depth. The hum-cancelling design ensures your performance is clean and noise-free while the integral thumb rest provides unrivalled playability.

Bridge
A high-mass die-cast bridge efficiently transfers string vibrations to the body for full, rich tone combined with excellent adjustability and road-tested durability. 18mm string spacing on the 5-string model offers outstanding playability for technical slap or finger styles.

FEATURES

Scale Length: 34'' (863.6mm)
Neck: Maple and Mahogany 5-piece bolt-on
Fingerboard: Rosewood
Radius: 23-5/8'' (600mm)
Nut Width: 43mm
Body: Solid Mahogany
Tuners: Die-cast
Bridge: Die-cast 18mm
Pickups: MHB3n (Ceramic),MHB3b (Ceramic)
Controls: Master Volume, Pickup Balancer, Bass, Treble and Performance EQ switch
Hardware Color: Black Nickel

item.title: Teisco Maier Kent 534 Basin St Bass 1960s Sunburst MIJ Japan — $500
item.description:
$500 · Good · Stokkers' Musical Instruments

1960s Maier 534 Basin Sunburst Bass

Awesome vintage made in Japan bass guitar!

This Maier bass was made by Teisco / Guyatone and would have been branded under many different names and models such as Kent Basin St Bass/534/634, Noble Big Beat B315 Super Rocket Bass, Lafayette, Sekova titan, etc.

This bass has a 33-1/2" scale length, a 1-3/4" nut width, a neck depth of .975" at the 1st and 5th fret, and 1.158" at the 12th fret, and weighs in at 8 lbs 13 oz.

The bass has been professionally set up with new Roto-Sound flatwound strings.

It comes with a new Gator Case gig bag.

The bass is used and shows signs of wear including nicks, dings, dents, scrapes, scratches, dirt, grime, bumps, and bruises, but is in otherwise great shape.

Please feel free to ask if you have any questions!

item.title: 2023 Fender Limited Edition Suona Jazz Bass Thinline - Violin Burst — $2,399.99
item.description:
$2,399.99 · Excellent · Leitz Music Company, Inc.

Leitz Music is a family owned and operated store on the gulf coast of Florida since 1970.  We are an authorized dealer for Gibson, Fender, Novo, Martin, Taylor, and more.

**These are not stock photos.  These are photographs of the actual instrument you’ll receive** 

Condition Notes:  This bass is in excellent players condition with very few visible signs of use.  There are marks on the pickguard from use as well as a small patch of lacquer haze from use on the trebel side above the pickup near the pickguard.  Guitar has light swirl marks on the top, but is otherwise super clean.  Guitar includes original case and case accessories show in the photos.  GUITAR WEIGHT:   7 Lbs 14oz

Instrument Specs: 

  • Semi-Hollow Ash Body with Bound Fiemme Valley Italian Alpine Spruce Top
  • Nitrocellulose Lacquer Violin Burst Finish with Matching Painted Headstock
  • Custom-Designed Suona Jazz Bass Pickups
  • Roasted Flame Maple Modern "C" Neck
  • 7.25"-9.5" Compound-Radius Ebony Fingerboard
  • HiMass™ Vintage 4-Saddle Bridge

*Setups and adjustments are the sole responsibility of the buyer*

SHIPPING INFORMATION:

  • NO RETURNS on International Orders or orders being sent to Freight Forwarding/ Export companies.
  • Shipping Rates are based on shipping box dimensions (DIM weight) not by shipping weight.
  • All new items are shipped in the same manner, box and materials in which the manufacturer/vendor ships them to us

  • Expedited shipping is available on our website at an additional cost.  Expedited shipping is not available on Reverb.com  orders.
  • All items over $200 will require a signature for delivery.
  • "Free Shipping" (if offered) only applies only to the contiguous 48 states, and not to Alaska, Hawaii, or Puerto Rico
  • Shipping For Returns Is The Sole Responsibility of The Buyer and will not be refunded

item.title: 2022 Squier Classic Vibe '70s Precision Bass Black w/Gig Bag #ISSA2200936 — $389
item.description:
$389 · Excellent · Thunder Road Guitars PDX

This 2022 Squier Classic Vibe '70s Precision Bass throws it back to the 1970s with a vintage-tint gloss neck finish and a vintage-style bridge with threaded saddles. It's a ton of fun to play and a great deal too!

The glossy black finish makes this bass susceptible to added reflections or light artifacts in photos, so please feel free to contact us with any questions you may have about its in-person condition. 

Freshly inspected and set up in-house by our esteemed Guitar Tech. In Excellent condition with minimal wear, including light scratches and other signs of normal handling and use. See below for more details and specifications.

_______________________________________________________________

SPECIFICATIONS:

Nickel Open Gear Tuners

1 5/8” Bone Nut 

Maple Neck with 34” Scale Length

Neck Thickness .81" at First Fret and .88" at Twelfth Fret

Maple Fingerboard with Block Inlays and 9.5" Radius

20 Medium Tall Frets in Excellent Condition

1 Alnico Split Single-Coil Pickup

Aluminum Four-Saddle Top-Loading Bridge

Nyatoh Body in Gloss Black Finish

Weight 8 lb 2 oz

Cahaya Gig Bag

Serial Number ISSA2200936

__________________________________________________________________

NOTE TO OUR VALUED CUSTOMERS:

We want you to be happy with your purchase and we try to represent the condition of all used and vintage guitars as accurately as possible in our listings. Every used guitar shows some signs of play wear and vintage models even more so. We call out all areas of damage or unusual wear, and describe the overall condition based upon what is "normal" for the age of the instrument. Expect to see minor dings, finish checking, or other evidence of normal use and aging which may not be captured by photography or explicitly mentioned in the listing.  

We recognize that everyone has their own personal standard of what constitutes acceptable wear and tear. If play wear is especially critical for you, please contact us to ask any questions you may have. Your satisfaction is of paramount importance to us.

If tweaking action, intonation, etc., having fret edges dressed, or having the instrument set up to your specs is not something you are comfortable with doing yourself, you will need the help of a local-to-you tech/professional. item.title: Schecter Guitar Research Omen Elite-4 4-String Electric Bass Guitar Regular See-Thru Blue Burst — $383.36
item.description:
$383.36 · Good · Musician's Friend

** Item Specific Details ** Neck has been repaired, visible at both sides of the neck at binding. ** The Schecter Omen Elite-4 bass guitar offers top-notch appointments at an affordable price. Featuring a mahogany body, an ultrafast maple neck and a 16" radius rosewood fingerboard, the Elite-4 bass is designed for speed and comfortable performance regardless of playing style.

Equipped with a calibrated set of Schecter Diamond Heretic J-Bucker pickups, this 4-string electric bass delivers earthshaking tone that is fully customizable with master volume, master tone and blend controls, along with a 3-way toggle that switches between series, parallel or split-coil settings. Additional features include Schecter bass tuners and a Schecter Custom bass bridge for enhanced tuning stability.

item.title: Fender Vintera II '60s Jazz Bass Regular Lake Placid Blue — $703.99
item.description:
$703.99 · Good · Musician's Friend

** Item Specific Details ** Has a dent on the side of the body on the bass side on the lower bout. Has scratches on the control plate. ** Revive the timeless sound of the '60s with the Fender Vintera II '60s Jazz Bass and experience the iconic looks, inspiring feel and incomparable tone that only a Fender can deliver.

The Vintera II '60s Jazz Bass features an alder body and a maple neck with rosewood fingerboard for classic Fender tone that's full of punch and clarity. The early-'60s C-shape neck is based on a traditional '60s profile for an intuitive and inviting feel, while the 7.25" radius fingerboard with vintage tall frets provide familiar comfort with ample room for big bends and expressive vibrato. Under the hood, the vintage-style early-'60s single-coil pickups deliver the legendary Fender sound: warm, woody, dynamic and powerful. The vintage-style 4-saddle bridge and tuning machines provide old-school looks with enhanced intonation and tuning stability to complete the package.

Experience the unmistakable feel and unmatched sound of a quintessential Fender with the Vintera II 60s Jazz Bass today and start making music history.

item.title: Used 2021 Fender Player Jazz Bass in Buttercream MX21103318 — $650
item.description:
$650 · Brand New · The Music Gallery of Highland Park

This Fender Player Jazz Bass in Buttercream is here on consignment and weighs 9 lbs 7 oz. The neck is straight, the frets are still crowned, and the truss rod functions properly.

The Player Series from Fender continues on the Standard Series Legacy! Offering considerable value, the New Fender Player Jazz Bass combines looks, specs, and playability, bringing things to a whole other level. 

This bass is built with an Body and then finished in Buttercream. The neck has just a slight tint to it so it isn't stark white. Other important neck specs include , 1.5" Nut Width, , and a satin urethane finish on the back of the neck. A 3 Ply Parchment pickguard helps give the bass a bit of a vintage vibe. 

A pair of Alnico V magnet-loaded pickups are installed. They have solid low end, crisp highs, and just enough scooped mids to make this bass sound powerful! Until you roll off the bridge pickup volume and tone down, and this thing sweetens up beautifully! Each pickup has its own volume control, and we have a Master Tone Control.

With its dual single-coil pickups and smooth playing feel, the Player Jazz Bass is an inspiring instrument with classic, elevated style, and authentic Fender bass tone. Its powerful, rumbling sound is punchy and tight; a growling voice that’s matched with a fast, smooth playing feel for a shot of creative inspiration. Ready for action in the studio, or on the stage, the Player Jazz Bass can take anything you can think of—and everything you haven’t yet. 

Features
  • Fender
  • Players Series
  • Buttercream Finish
  • Alder Body
  • Weighs 9 lbs 7 oz
  • Maple Neck/Fingerboard
  • Modern C-Shaped Neck
  • Satin on Back/Gloss Fingerboard
  • 34" Scale
  • 9.5" Radius
  • 20 Medium Jumbo Frets

  • Players Series Alnico V Jazz Bass Pickups

item.title: Fender Player II Mustang PJ Bass - Cactus Gray — $849.99
item.description:
$849.99 · Brand New · Downtown Sounds Workers Cooperative, Inc

Step into the spotlight and experience iconic Fender® sound and style with the Player II Mustang® Bass PJ — a stage-ready bass guitar with contemporary updates to power your performance and inspire your playing.

The Player II Mustang® Bass PJ radiates timeless Fender charm, but under the hood, it's primed for today's players. Everything about the neck is designed for fast and fluid playability, from the Modern “C”-profile with silky satin urethane finish on the back to the comfy 9.5”-radius slab rosewood or maple fingerboard with smooth rolled edges and 19 medium jumbo frets. A classic alder body is available in both timeless Fender finishes and never-before-seen colors unearthed from the archives. Player Series Alnico V Split-Coil P bass® and Single-Coil J bass® pickups offer sweet highs, growling mids and seismic lows that elevate any genre. The 3-way toggle switch lets you easily dial in everything this versatile pickup set has to offer, while an adjustable 4-saddle bridge, single-groove steel saddles and open-gear tuners ensure precise tuning stability for the flexibility to explore endless sonic possibilities.

Perfect for crafting your own sound, the Player II Mustang® Bass PJ has the looks, tone and feel that only a Fender delivers.

Alder Body
Modern "C" Neck Profile
9.5“-Radius Maple or Rosewood Fingerboard with Rolled Edges
Player Series Alnico V Split-Coil Precision Bass® and Alnico V Single-Coil Jazz Bass® Pickups

item.title: Vintage Vox Bill Wyman Bass Cherry 1960s — $2,499
item.description:
$2,499 · Very Good · Cream City Music

Now here's a rare find - a late 1960's Vox Wyman Bass finished in cherry! We typically see these in sunburst, making it the perfect addition to any vintage aficionado's collection - especially if you're a Stones fan! Fully inspected & set up ready to play straight out of the case.

Cosmetic Condition: Pretty good shape actually for being well over 50 years old - various nicks, chips, dents & dings; scratches. weather checking & some wear on the back pad. Overall the bass has a wonderful, true vintage look to it with a gorgeous patina.

Neck: Super skinny maple neck with cream binding & black side-dots. Rosewood fingerboard with mother of pearl dot inlays. Frets are in great shape with hardly any wear. Open-gear nickel tuners & single string tree. 1 7/16" nut width with zero fret; 30" scale length. Neck is straight & truss rod works fine.

Body: Teardrop shape, hollow maple body in cherry finish. Bound top & back with bound F-hole. Top-loading bridge with two adjustable threaded saddles & original bridge cover. Chrome "mirror" pickguard with Vox logo. The original back pad is "torn & frayed" a bit, but all the snaps are there. Bass weighs 6 lbs 5.5 oz.

Electronics: Original single coils with white covers & chrome bezels. Master volume & tone controls with 3-way selector switch. All electronics are in perfect working condition.

Case: Original hardshell case included.

item.title: Yamaha BB735A-DCS - Dark Coffee Sunburst — $759.99
item.description:
$759.99 · B-Stock · GuitarSloth

Yamaha BB735A 5-String Electric Bass Guitar – Dark Coffee Sunburst

The Yamaha BB735A is a professional-level 5-string bass designed for players who demand versatility, reliability, and exceptional tone. Built on Yamaha’s legendary BB platform, the BB735A combines classic bass character with modern innovations that enhance sustain, playability, and tonal flexibility.

The alder/maple/alder multi-laminate body delivers a balanced tone with powerful low end, clear mids, and excellent projection. Yamaha’s 6-bolt miter neck joint improves vibration transfer between the neck and body, providing enhanced sustain and resonance. The Vintage Plus bridge with diagonal body-through stringing further increases string vibration transfer while offering adjustable tonal options.

Featuring Yamaha’s YGD Custom V7 Alnico V pickups, the BB735A delivers a rich, organic tone with excellent definition. The active/passive electronics provide a wide range of sounds, with automatic level matching so you can switch between modes without unwanted volume changes. Active mode includes a powerful 3-band EQ for precise tone shaping, while passive mode offers a more traditional feel with the treble control functioning as a master tone.

The Vintage Plus convertible bridge allows for additional customization with multiple stringing options and adjustable saddles that provide two different tonal characteristics. Stainless steel hardware helps protect against corrosion, ensuring this bass is built for years of dependable performance.

Key Features:

  • 5-string design with powerful low-end response
  • Alder/maple/alder multi-laminate body for balanced tone and resonance
  • 6-bolt miter neck joint for improved sustain and vibration transfer
  • Yamaha YGD Custom V7 Alnico V pickups for rich, defined tone
  • Active/passive electronics with automatic level matching
  • 3-band active EQ for extensive tone shaping
  • Pickup blend control for precise tonal adjustment
  • Vintage Plus bridge with diagonal body-through stringing
  • Convertible saddles for two distinct tonal options
  • Brass saddles and steel bridge plate for balanced response
  • Stainless steel hardware for long-term durability
  • Lightweight tuners for improved balance

This item is a B-Stock guitar sourced directly from the manufacturer. It is new and has never been previously owned. The photos shown are of the exact instrument you will receive.

Before shipment, our licensed technician will inspect and professionally set up the guitar to ensure it is playing its best

During our inspection, we noticed a chip in the finish on back. (see last pictures)

Please note, these may not be the only cosmetic imperfections. As a B-Stock instrument, there may be additional minor blemishes that are small in nature and do not affect playability or performance.

See more and check us out direct at cahousemusic.com

item.title: Fender American Vintage II 1966 Jazz Bass®, Rosewood Fingerboard, Lake Placid Blue — $2,699.99
item.description:
$2,699.99 · Brand New · Remix Music

Fender American Vintage II 1966 Jazz Bass®, Rosewood Fingerboard, Lake Placid Blue...

The Fender® American Vintage II series presents a remarkably accurate take on the revolutionary designs that altered the course of musical history.
Built with period-accurate bodies, necks and hardware, premium finishes and meticulously voiced, year-specific pickups, each instrument captures
the essence of authentic Fender craftsmanship and tone.

When Don Randall at Fender Sales requested a more sophisticated, high-end bass model, the now iconic Jazz Bass® was the result. The neck was narrower
at the nut, providing a faster and more effortless feel and the sleek new body style mirrored the offset contoured style of the Jazzmaster®. By 1966,
evolution of the instrument reached new heights with striking new features.

The American Vintage II 1966 Jazz Bass features a comfortable "U" shape hard rock maple neck, and it plays like a dream with a 7.25" radius round-lam
rosewood fingerboard and vintage tall frets. Characteristic of production in 1966, this J-Bass® is offered with white neck binding, pearl dots, two
volume controls with master tone, matching painted headstock and 4-ply tortoiseshell pickguard. The 4-saddle bridge with threaded saddles, vintage-style
"lollipop" tuners and Pure Vintage '66 Jazz Bass pickups deliver classic thump and style.

The instruments in the American Vintage II series are direct descendants of the original Fenders: designed for players with a fine appreciation for vintage
Fender tone and feel and built with unmatched quality, down to the last screw. These are Fender electrics in their purest form: Fender American Vintage II,
the stuff of legends.

Features:
Alder Body
Gloss Nitrocellulose Lacquer Finish
Pure Vintage '66 Single-Coil Jazz Bass® Pickups
"U"-Shaped Neck Profile
Bound Round-Lam Rosewood Fretboard with Paint Matched Headstock
Pure Vintage 'Lollipop' Tuners

item.title: 1968 Gibson EB-2 DC- Sparkling Burgundy — $5,606.15
item.description:
$5,606.15 · Excellent · L’Atelier L’Hirondelle

⚡️1968 Gibson EB-2 DC Sparkling Burgundy, with case ⚡️. We brought this bass in from afar; it is a rare find in absolutely remarkable condition.

This is the dual-pickup hollowbody model, featuring a large PAF-style pickup in the neck position and a mini-humbucker at the bridge for added versatility.

It has two volume controls, two tone controls, and a "baritone" switch that produces a very interesting phasing effect unique to this model.

This Gibson sports a "custom color" finish—Sparkling Burgundy—which is very rare and similar to Fender’s Candy Apple Red. It has taken on a slight coppery hue over time—simply magnificent.

Note the superb rosewood fretboard and original frets in excellent condition. It plays incredibly well; the neck is straight and feels great, the action is low, and the playing dynamics are amazing.

I’ve inspected and set it up in the workshop, and flatwound strings suit it perfectly.
It has been played, yet clearly spent many years safe and sound in its case under the owner's bed. As a result, it has aged beautifully and is now ready to hit the stage again.

A legendary, rare bass—and above all, a superb instrument that we are thrilled to offer here at the workshop!
Enjoy!
